page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 192 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in both single-core and multi-core CPU execution, with stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests ranking in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, which is reflected in a strong Passmark CPU encryption score. Memory performance is also robust, with Passmark memory scores in the top 25%, though uncached memory bandwidth reads and writes remain in the middle 50%. Ray tracing and image processing benchmarks also show strong results, while database operations, static web serving, and Redis SET operations perform in the average tier.

This configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, image processing, and moderate-scale text generation using smaller language models. The lack of local NVMe storage means that high-throughput database operations will rely entirely on network-attached storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. The server offers balanced resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for general-purpose enterprise applications, CI/CD pipelines, and web serving environments that require dedicated CPU resources without the premium cost of specialized memory or GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. This server provides dedicated CPU allocation across 24 physical cores with multithreading. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, encryption capabilities, and memory scores, placing it in the top 25% for compute-heavy tasks like ray tracing and image processing. Conversely, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. This balanced resource profile makes the n2d-standard-48 highly suitable for enterprise web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose multi-threaded workloads where dedicated CPU performance is required without the added cost of specialized hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-48 features 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering strong multi-core compute for general workloads.
tagline: A balanced 48-vCPU AMD EPYC server offering strong multi-core performance and dedicated resources for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance in the top 25%
No local storage or GPU resources included in this configuration
Optimized for software compilation, image processing, and web serving
Dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance for enterprise applications
